excerpt: He saw, he thought, he withdrew, and he knew.
source_location_hint: Lectures of al-Muʾayyad bi-Allāh on cosmological emanation

excerpt: The relationship to his Originator
source_location_hint: Doctrinal exposition of the First Intellect's recognition of the Creator

excerpt: Performance of negation and affirmation of divinity, glorification and sanctification
source_location_hint: Taʾwīl of the First Intellect's monotheistic act

excerpt: Allah bears witness that there is none worthy of worship save Him
source_location_hint: Testimony to divine singularity

excerpt: Intellect is the binding and restraint of the self within the commandments and boundaries
source_location_hint: Etymology of ʿaql in mystical context

excerpt: In the knowledge of God, the First Intellect and the Respondent stand equal, for both are unable to fully comprehend Him
source_work: Khunūz al-Ḥikmatayn al-Madhkūra li-Arbāb al-Asmāʾ (Treasures of Wisdom for the Masters of the Names)
source_author: al-Sayyid ʿAlī ibn Hibat Allāh al-Hamdānī
source_location_hint: Doctrine of equal incapacity before the Creator

excerpt: Quiddity refers to origin, interior, or essential reality of divine knowledge
source_location_hint: Distinction between quantity and quiddity in the First Intellect

excerpt: The First Intellect has been described as the Holy Spirit (rūḥ al-Quds)
source_location_hint: Mystical nomenclature of the First Intellect

excerpt: Upon receiving the reward of monotheism, the First Intellect trembled with joy immeasurable
source_location_hint: Description of the First Intellect's ecstatic joy

excerpt: Example of the exalted and lower gazes in the context of worship and precedence
source_location_hint: Exemplification of intention and action in monotheism

excerpt: Deeds are valued by their intentions; the intention of a believer speaks louder than his action
source_location_hint: Primacy of intention in worship

excerpt: The practical form eclipsed the scientific form through vocalization of the Word
source_location_hint: Doctrine of scientific and practical worship

excerpt: Through the definite article, the divine becomes Allah, and through secondary perfection, becomes chieftaincy
source_location_hint: Ontological elevation through divine nomination

excerpt: Through attainment of sacred matter, he attained elevation, greatness, majesty, blessedness, and immaculacy
source_location_hint: Attributes of the First Intellect after secondary perfection

excerpt: The First Intellect became the First and the Last, and none can ever remove him from his station
source_work: Tanbih al-Hadi (Guidance of the Rightly Guided)
source_location_hint: Eternal primacy of the First Intellect

excerpt: The ninety-nine attributes of Allah manifest in their entirety within the First Intellect
source_location_hint: Divine names and the First Intellect

excerpt: Upon this the number is founded, and from it enumeration flows
source_location_hint: Doctrine of numerical emanation from the First Intellect

excerpt: Through the acquisition of sacred matter, ten intellects were compiled
source_location_hint: The emanation of the ten intellects

excerpt: He restrained himself from what was not his, denying divinity from his own essence
source_location_hint: Definition of wisdom in the First Intellect

excerpt: He first gained precedence over his own kind in the deed of monotheism
source_location_hint: Primacy of the First Intellect among equals

excerpt: The seven intellects have been named words because of their relation to the First Intellect; those intellects were meaningless letters until the First Intellect organized them into meaningful words
source_work: Jamiʿ al-Hikmatayn (The Concourse of Two Wisdoms)
source_location_hint: The Word and the naming of subsequent intellects
